text.title: Burn-rate Multi-window Alerting Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `Burn-rate Multi-window Alerting` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every page alert MUST pair a short window (e.g. 1h, 5m) with a long window (e.g. 6h, 30m); single-window pages are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Single-window alerts either alert too late (large window) or page on noise (small window); the pair filters both.

rule.statement: Page burn-rate MUST consume ≥ 2% of the 30-day error budget within the alert window. Ticket burn-rate MUST consume ≥ 5% within 6h.
rule.rationale: Below these thresholds the response cost outweighs the budget impact.

rule.statement: Burn-rate query MUST be expressed as `bad_events / total_events`, not raw error count. Raw counts ignore traffic shape.
rule.rationale: Raw counts mask the real user-impact ratio under traffic spikes.

rule.statement: Burn-rate fractions MUST be pre-computed via recording rules (e.g. `slo:burnrate1h`) — alerts do not run multi-minute range queries inline.
rule.rationale: Inline burn-rate queries blow up Prometheus eval cost on large multi-tenant fleets.

rule.statement: Burn-rate thresholds + window pair MUST be reviewed quarterly against the prior cycle's alert outcomes (pages → real incidents ratio).
rule.rationale: Static thresholds drift from real-world signal quality; quarterly review keeps page precision honest.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
